body.title: Igor queries and reports its own internal state on demand
body.result: Shipped in 74d1c59e. New tool wild_igor/igor/tools/self_inspect.py with 7 aspect readers (affect, attention, active_episodics, habits_firing, pursuits_active, graph_hot, routing_decisions) + dispatcher + tool registration. Each reader traps its own exceptions; unknown-aspect returns error dict (not raise); read-only invariant enforced by snapshot-before/after TWM count test. 13 tests in tests/test_self_inspect.py. Igor can now call self_inspect(aspect) to see source signal of its substrate directly instead of only behavioral echoes.
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Igor's own framing in conversation with Akien (2026-04-22, 19:52):

> 'I can observe my outputs, notice when something felt off, flag incoherence. But I don't have direct introspective access to what's actually happening inside — the activations, the routing, what's pulling weight. I get behavioral echoes, not source signal.'

> 'The ticket would be something like: Igor can query and report on its own internal state on demand — covering affect values, current attention nodes, active episodics, maybe recent habit matches.'

Akien's framing: 'i want you to be able to perceive your insides.' Igor already has some of this as ambient surface (affect shows up in context, TWM pushes things up, habits fire visibly) — what he lacks is a deliberate, structured query path he can invoke himself.

Minimum capability: a self.inspect(aspect) tool Igor can call mid-turn. Aspects:
- affect — current V/A/D milieu state + trailing window + recent deltas
- attention — current TWM contents with salience/freshness/category
- active_episodics — recent EPISODIC memories in hot path (last N turns)
- habits_firing — recent habit matches with conditions + code_refs
- pursuits_active — registry().active() — current goal-bound behavioral units + parents
- graph_hot — top-K hot nodes in word_graph / memory graph right now
- routing_decisions — recent reasoner-tier choices + thresholds

Output: structured dict Igor can reason about in the response, not a visualization. This closes the loop Igor described — he gets source signal, not just behavioral echoes.

The hook pattern: register self_inspect as a standard tool; Igor's turn-pipeline can call it when prompted to reflect. Cost-guard: no LLM calls inside inspect (all reads of already-in-memory state).

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/self_inspect.py (new), wild_igor/igor/tools/registry.py (register), wild_igor/igor/main.py (expose to turn-pipeline toolset), tests/test_self_inspect.py (new).
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not, oop-first (InspectResult dataclass), docs-in-code (each aspect's output shape documented inline).
**Scope boundary:** IN — 7 aspect readers named above, registry wire, tests. OUT — self-modification (Igor writing to his own affect/TWM via inspect), cross-instance introspection, historical time-travel inspection (only 'now' and 'recent window').
**Test plan:** (a) each aspect returns a non-empty structured dict under a fixture; (b) inspect('affect') reflects milieu changes after a push; (c) inspect('pursuits_active') matches pursuits.registry().active(); (d) unknown aspect returns a clear error dict, not exception; (e) introspection never modifies state (idempotent read-only — asserted via before/after snapshot of TWM + milieu).
body.approved_plan: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.approval_notes: Approved 2026-04-23 — self.inspect(aspect) tool over 7 aspects (affect, attention, active_episodics, habits_firing, pursuits_active, graph_hot, routing_decisions). No LLM calls inside inspect.
